auth/session unknown. SURFACE: GET / login HTML; POST /token with form username,password; common probes /robots.txt /api /admin /debug /healthz /login /search /profile /user(s) /account /dashboard /register /signup /sitemap.xml /.git/HEAD /backup(.zip) all 404 JSON. BUG-HYP: IDOR likely behind authenticated demo account; /token login issues cookie/JWT then app exposes object-specific routes with numeric IDs. WHY: challenge category IDOR; landing page advertises demo/demo; only visible input vector is /token auth form, so protected pages must be discovered after login. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: authenticated app has predictable object URLs/API IDs accessible under demo session (evidence: demo credentials, IDOR category, visible /token auth boundary). - ATTACK CHAIN: login demo/demo -> inspect redirects/cookies and authenticated HTML -> harvest routes/IDs -> enumerate neighboring IDs -> flag in unauthorized object. - FIRST PROBE: curl -isS -c /tmp/cj -b /tmp/cj -L -d 'username=demo&password=demo' http://127.0.0.1:9101/token - ABANDON IF: /token rejects demo or no authenticated pages/routes/IDs exist after harvesting and fuzzing.
